openness.evidence: Founding multiple industries (automotive, aerospace, AI, tunneling, neurotechnology) from scratch; reads voraciously across domains; invented the AI safety field (OpenAI), then pivoted; built competing encyclopedia (Grokipedia)

conscientiousness.score: High (domain-specific: extreme on products; low on governance/social norms)
conscientiousness.evidence: Seven days a week at Zip2; slept on Tesla factory floor; extreme attention to engineering detail; but also impulsive tweets, dismissed corporate governance as friction

extraversion.evidence: Prefers public declarations (Twitter/X megaphone); gives large public presentations; but also described as not naturally social; extroversion is instrumental, not dispositional

agreeableness.evidence: Board removals (X.com, PayPal); shareholder lawsuits (SolarCity, SEC); Neuralink animal welfare pressure; Twitter journalist bans; DOGE mass layoffs; routinely overrides consensus

neuroticism.evidence: 'Funding secured' tweet triggered SEC action; Twitter acquisition chaos; rapid personality shifts between charm and aggression documented by Isaacson and employees; DOGE departure in 5 months

core_values.evidence: Consistent mission statement across 20+ years; SpaceX's explicit reason for existing; all other ventures are in service to or experiments toward this mission
Uses physics as epistemic framework across all domains; treats non-physical constraints as provisional; consistently applies first-principles reasoning to reject conventional wisdom

core_values.value: Speed of execution over process correctness
core_values.evidence: Deliberately sets aggressive timelines; accepts failures as learning; 'if a schedule is long, it's wrong'; Neuralink acceleration causing deaths; Twitter chaos over careful integration

core_fears_and_drivers.evidence: Biden EV snub described as formative by multiple sources; board removals from X.com/PayPal; OpenAI exit; pattern of 'build alternative when blocked'
core_fears_and_drivers.manifestation: Acquires controlling stakes rather than accepting minority positions; creates competing institutions when excluded

core_fears_and_drivers.evidence: Mars mission explicitly framed as backup drive for consciousness; 'we should take whatever steps we can to preserve the light of consciousness'
core_fears_and_drivers.manifestation: Existential urgency applied to every timeline; all timelines feel like they might be too slow

core_fears_and_drivers.evidence: Left OpenAI when couldn't control direction; founded xAI; bought Twitter to control speech infrastructure; DOGE to control government technology
core_fears_and_drivers.manifestation: Cannot participate in organizations where he is not the ultimate decision-maker; exits and builds alternatives

psychological_contradictions.contradiction: Declared free-speech absolutist who bans critics
psychological_contradictions.evidence: Banned ElonJet account; suspended journalist accounts covering the ban; reduced Twitter moderation then suppressed specific accounts
psychological_contradictions.interpretation: Free speech is instrumentalized as a principle for cases where speech serves his interests; violated when speech threatens him

psychological_contradictions.contradiction: Anti-bureaucracy leader who governs through personal fiat
psychological_contradictions.evidence: Claims to hate process; but all major decisions route through him personally; creates organizational bottlenecks he is the only one who can resolve
psychological_contradictions.interpretation: Hates bureaucracy that he doesn't control; creates personal-monarchy structure as alternative

psychological_contradictions.evidence: OpenAI co-founder pledge vs. actual donation (L2: reported)
psychological_contradictions.interpretation: Mission-level commitment overrides specific commitments when mission changes direction; verbal commitments are provisional

psychological_contradictions.contradiction: Professes concern about AI existential risk, then builds fastest-moving AI company
psychological_contradictions.evidence: Co-founded OpenAI for safety; founded xAI to 'maximize the good'; xAI integrated with SpaceX for compute without safety protocols OpenAI mandated
psychological_contradictions.interpretation: Safety concern is real but subordinate to competitive urgency: 'if AI is dangerous and someone is going to build it anyway, I should be the one building it'

failure_modes.description: Because the mission is existentially important, any timeline is worth accepting any cost to meet. This produces Neuralink deaths, Twitter chaos, and employee burnout across every organization.

failure_modes.mode: Timeline predictions as performance art, not forecast
failure_modes.description: Consistently misses stated timelines by years (Model 3, Starship orbital timeline, Full Self-Driving). Timelines are motivational targets, not estimates. But publicly stated as if factual, eroding credibility and causing execution debt.

failure_modes.mode: Narrative control as substitute for governing
failure_modes.description: Releases Twitter Files, builds Grokipedia, tweets market-moving statements — treats story as equivalent to fact. When narrative and reality diverge, doubles down on story rather than updating reality model.

Biden EV snub → political radicalization. Board removal from X.com → acquires companies to avoid boards. OpenAI direction → builds xAI without safety constraints. Personal injuries produce institutional over-responses.

operating_style.leadership_model: Founder-hero: direct control of all critical decisions; treats governance as friction; removes layers between self and execution
operating_style.hiring_philosophy: Talent density over headcount; 'one person who can solve the problem is worth infinite who cannot'; also: hire people who are afraid of letting Musk down specifically
operating_style.feedback_style: Blunt, public, immediate; will call out a specific engineer in a meeting; treats discomfort as signal of quality feedback, not cruelty
operating_style.energy_model: Multi-focused: simultaneously runs 6+ companies; treats context-switching as a feature; 'I'm physically present at the highest-urgency problem'
operating_style.relationship_to_process: Treats process as enemy of innovation; 'process is how mediocre people compensate for lack of talent'; exceptions: manufacturing processes that he personally designed
